_comment: Atlas demo corpus. Five named connectors across TWO industries (a manufacturing pump + an IT datacenter) so the demo shows the system is generalized, not hardcoded. It exercises: cross-source entity resolution (shared serial/asset_tag), anomaly gating (a benign log that must NOT become a failure), single-asset RCA (pump bearing), multi-hop RCA (db symptom -> upstream switch fault via depends_on), and compliance (a compliant asset with a completed work order vs an at-risk asset with none). Connector 'description' is chosen so relation_from_description maps each source to the right event kind.

connectors.name: Plant Asset Register
connectors.description: Master asset register and equipment catalog

records.asset_name: Feed pump P-101 — Grundfos CR-64 on Line 3
Database server db-prod-02 — Dell PowerEdge R760
Core aggregation switch sw-core-7 — Cisco Nexus 9300

connectors.name: CMMS Work Orders
connectors.description: CMMS work order and maintenance history (Maximo)

records.description: Replaced mechanical seal and re-aligned coupling on feed pump P-101
Topped up bearing lubrication and logged a follow-up vibration check
Vibration alarm and rising bearing temperature on the feed pump; discharge flow fell roughly 18% over two hours
Scheduled lubrication completed on the feed pump; vibration and temperature returned to baseline, no action required
Intermittent packet loss and elevated round-trip latency to db-prod-02 during peak hours; CPU, memory and disk all within normal range
Uplink port flapping on the core aggregation switch with repeated interface resets and CRC errors
db-prod-02 sits behind and depends on core aggregation switch sw-core-7

connectors.name: Compliance Register
connectors.description: Compliance and permit register of audit rules

records.description: Rotating equipment must complete a documented quarterly vibration inspection
Production database servers must complete quarterly security patching
